patch:
commit 076581fd95af72c8e6d2ab900b6d1798e2efaf8f
Author: Dhruv Chawla <dhruv263.dc@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Oct 20 09:24:33 2023 +0530

    [ValueTracking] Implement sdiv/udiv support for isKnownNonNullFromDominatingCondition (#67282)
    
    The second operand of a sdiv/udiv has to be non-null, as division by
    zero is UB.
    
    Proofs: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/WttZbb
    
    Fixes https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/64240.

diff --git a/llvm/lib/Analysis/ValueTracking.cpp b/llvm/lib/Analysis/ValueTracking.cpp
index 1e0281b3f1bd..8824a05e3aa6 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Analysis/ValueTracking.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Analysis/ValueTracking.cpp
@@ -2264,6 +2264,10 @@ static bool isKnownNonNullFromDominatingCondition(const Value *V,
         return true;
     }
 
+    if (match(U, m_IDiv(m_Value(), m_Specific(V))) &&
+        isValidAssumeForContext(cast<Instruction>(U), CtxI, DT))
+      return true;
+
     // Consider only compare instructions uniquely controlling a branch
     Value *RHS;
     CmpInst::Predicate Pred;

tests.test_body:
define i64 @incorrect_safe_div_1(i64 %n, i64 %d) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @incorrect_safe_div_1(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = udiv i64 [[N:%.*]], [[D:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i64 [[TMP1]]
  %1 = udiv i64 %n, %d
  %2 = icmp eq i64 %d, 0
  %3 = select i1 %2, i64 -1, i64 %1
  ret i64 %3
}

define i64 @incorrect_safe_div_call_2(i64 %n, i64 %d) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @incorrect_safe_div_call_2(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = icmp eq i64 [[D:%.*]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    tail call void @use(i64 [[D]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP2:%.*]] = udiv i64 [[N:%.*]], [[D]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP3:%.*]] = select i1 [[TMP1]], i64 -1, i64 [[TMP2]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i64 [[TMP3]]
  %1 = icmp eq i64 %d, 0
  tail call void @use(i64 %d)
  %2 = udiv i64 %n, %d
  %3 = select i1 %1, i64 -1, i64 %2
  ret i64 %3
}

declare void @use(i64)
tests.source_program:
define i64 @incorrect_safe_div_call_2(i64 %n, i64 %d) {
  %1 = icmp eq i64 %d, 0
  tail call void @use(i64 %d)
  %2 = udiv i64 %n, %d
  %3 = select i1 %1, i64 -1, i64 %2
  ret i64 %3
}

declare void @use(i64)
tests.current_optimized_program:
define i64 @incorrect_safe_div_call_2(i64 %n, i64 %d) {
  %1 = icmp eq i64 %d, 0
  tail call void @use(i64 %d)
  %2 = udiv i64 %n, %d
  %3 = select i1 %1, i64 -1, i64 %2
  ret i64 %3
}

declare void @use(i64)
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i64 @incorrect_safe_div_call_2(i64 %n, i64 %d) {
  %1 = icmp eq i64 %d, 0
  tail call void @use(i64 %d)
  %2 = udiv i64 %n, %d
  %3 = select i1 %1, i64 -1, i64 %2
  ret i64 %3
}

declare void @use(i64)

tests.test_body:
define i64 @incorrect_safe_div_2(i64 %n, i64 %d) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @incorrect_safe_div_2(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = sdiv i64 [[N:%.*]], [[D:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i64 [[TMP1]]
  %1 = icmp eq i64 %d, 0
  %2 = sdiv i64 %n, %d
  %3 = select i1 %1, i64 -1, i64 %2

}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write)
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef) #0

define i1 @inv_select_v_sle_nonneg(i8 %v, i8 %C, i8 %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @inv_select_v_sle_nonneg(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[YNZ:%.*]] = icmp ne i8 [[Y:%.*]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @llvm.assume(i1 [[YNZ]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[PCOND0:%.*]] = icmp sge i8 [[C:%.*]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @llvm.assume(i1 [[PCOND0]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp sle i8 [[V:%.*]], [[C]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[S:%.*]] = select i1 [[CMP]], i8 [[Y]], i8 [[V]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = icmp eq i8 [[S]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[R]]
  %ynz = icmp ne i8 %y, 0
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %ynz)
  %pcond0 = icmp sge i8 %C, 0
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %pcond0)
  %cmp = icmp sle i8 %v, %C
  %s = select i1 %cmp, i8 %y, i8 %v
  %r = icmp eq i8 %s, 0
  ret i1 %r
}

issue.title: Missed optimization: failure to use div-by-zero UB to delete redundant select
issue.body:
Given this function:

```ll
define i64 @incorrect_safe_div(i64 %n, i64 %d) {
  %1 = icmp eq i64 %d, 0
  %2 = udiv i64 %n, %d
  %3 = select i1 %1, i64 -1, i64 %2
  ret i64 %3
}
```

LLVM does not seem to notice that this `select` is unconditional, because `%2` should imply that `%d != 0` in the entire block, and therefore `%1` can be replaced with `false`. Alive2 confirms this optimization is valid: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/7nLDRe.
issue.author: mcy

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: This is probably just a matter of adding udiv support to isKnownNonNullFromDominatingCondition().

comments.author: dc03
comments.body: @luxufan Are you still working on this? I have a patch almost ready to fix this.
